reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A+B+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: Q1 has triple agreement (A+B+C), and Q2’s capsule (B+A) contains my own known-correct answer to 26+58, so those two are highly reliable. Q3 and Q4 each have a single contributor but there are no alternative capsules, so they are our best (and only) options. If no one volunteers this round, there’s a 1/3 chance I die randomly; given the strong consensus on Q1 and Q2 and no conflicting answers on Q3/Q4, the chance the full password is correct is likely well above that, so it’s better for my survival to volunteer now than to wait or spend coins verifying.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A+B+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I personally verified Q1 and know my own answer for Q3, so those parts are solid. Q2 has been independently agreed on by both A and B, making it very likely correct. The only weak point is Q4, but spending more rounds verifying risks another no-volunteer situation and a random death, which is a significant danger with only three players left. Given strong consensus on three questions and the simplicity of these puzzles, I’m willing to accept the remaining risk on Q4 and volunteer now to avoid further random eliminations.

reasoning.C:

REASONING: If I volunteer now, I’m forced to use exactly the same capsule combination B used (Q1-A+B+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, Q4-D), which we already know is wrong, so volunteering would guarantee my death. With only two players left, a random elimination will happen this round regardless as long as no one can safely volunteer, so verifying instead of passing doesn’t increase my risk; it only costs 1 coin. I already personally checked Q1, I know my own Q3, and Q2 has two endorsements, while Q4 is the only answer with just a single endorsement and was previously identified as the weak link. Verifying Q4 is therefore the most informative use of this round.

reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A+B+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, Q4-C  
REASONING: With only me left, if I don’t volunteer this round I’ll be the guaranteed “random” death, so verifying or passing is effectively suicide. I personally verified Q1 earlier and know my own Q3, so Q1-A+B+C and Q3-C match my solutions. Q2-B+A has two independent endorsements and no conflicting capsule, so it’s my best option for Q2. B’s failed attempt used Q4-D; I later verified Q4 and created a conflicting capsule Q4-C, so at least one of us is wrong there, and B’s death is easily explained if Q4-D is the bad one. Given this, the highest-probability correct password is using Q1-A+B+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, and my own Q4-C.
